title: Unity says its AI tech will soon eliminate the need for coding
text:
Programming
Production
Business
News
Unity says its AI tech will soon be able to 'prompt full casual games into existence'
Company CEO Matt Bromberg claims AI will empower non-coders and seasoned developers alike.
Chris Kerr
,
Senior Editor, News
,
GameDeveloper.com
February 16, 2026
2 Min Read
Image via Unity
Engine maker Unity hasn't been shy about throwing its weight behind generative AI tools in a purported bid to help its users
boost discoverability
,
generate advertising revenue
, and
accelerate product innovation
The tech has also allowed Unity to
publicly embarrass itself
on at least one occasion.
Still, Unity remains committed to the
divisive technology
and has now suggested its AI offering will soon be capable of prompting entire video games into being.
Discussing the state of the company's AI business on a
recent earnings call
, Unity CEO Matthew Bromberg said the company is preparing to unveil a new Unity AI beta at GDC Festival of Gaming in March that will negate the need for coding.
"AI-driven authoring is our second major area of focus for 2026," said Bromberg. "At the Game Developer Conference in March, we'll be unveiling a beta of the new upgraded Unity AI, which will enable developers to prompt full casual games into existence with natural language only, native to our platform—so it's simple to move from prototype to finished product."
Related:
Video codecs are a nightmare for game developers, but there's no solution in sight
"This assistant will be powered by our unique understanding of the project context and our runtime, while leveraging the best frontier models that exist. We believe together this combination will provide more efficient, more effective results to game developers than general-purpose models alone."
Bromberg claimed Unity AI will "democratize" game development for non-coders while raising productivity for all users.
"Our goal is to remove as much friction from the creative process as possible, becoming the universal bridge between the first spark of creativity and a successful, scalable, and enduring digital experience," he continued.
During the Q&A portion of the earnings call, Bromberg suggested there will eventually be "tens of millions of more people creating interactive entertainment" driven by AI-enabled development tools, and emphasized that Unity intends to lead the charge in that regard.
As noted in a legal explainer,
the Unity AI assistant currently uses large language models from OpenAI and Meta (GPT and Llama, respectively) to answer user questions, generate code, and run agentic actions.
Unity AI generators leverage several first-party and partner models to produce and refine assets—with those partners including Scenario (trained on Stable Diffusion, FLUX, Bria, and GPT-Image foundation models) and Layer AI (based on Stable Diffusion and FLUX foundation models).

Unity

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton.
